title: Xanthomyrtus scolopacina
intro: Xanthomyrtus scolopacina is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the 'Wild Orange'. The health benefits of Xanthomyrtus scolopacina include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Xanthomyrtus scolopacina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.

The classification of Xanthomyrtus scolopacina is closely related to its evolutionary history and morphological characteristics. uses: Xanthomyrtus scolopacina has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its potential health benefits.
